300: Rise of an Empire (2014)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $106,580,051Details
International Box Office $224,200,000
Worldwide Box Office $330,780,051
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $17,037,973
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $17,612,690
Total $34,650,663
Further financial details...

Synopsis

This new chapter of the epic saga takes the action to a fresh battlefield—on the sea—as Greek general Themistokles attempts to unite all of Greece by leading the charge that will change the course of the war. This pits Themistokles against the massive invading Persian forces led by mortal-turned-god Xerxes, and Artemisia, vengeful commander of the Persian navy.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$45,038,460 (42.3% of total gross)
Legs:2.37 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:32.2% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$110,000,000 (worldwide box office is 3.0 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,470 opening theaters/3,490 max. theaters, 4.6 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $156,021,716

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: March 7th, 2014 (Wide) by Warner Bros.
March 7th, 2014 (IMAX) by Warner Bros.
International Releases: March 7th, 2014 (Wide) (Italy)
June 20th, 2014 (Wide) (Japan)
Video Release: June 24th, 2014 by Warner Home Video
September 29th, 2014 by Warner Home Video
MPA Rating: R for strong sustained sequences of stylized bloody violence throughout, a sex scene, nudity and some language.
(Rating bulletin 2284, 8/7/2013)
Running Time: 102 minutes
Franchise: 300
Cast, crew, or production detail: Legacy Sequel, Sequels Without Their Original Stars
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Post-production Conversion, IMAX: DMR
Plot point: War
Social setting: Sword and Sandal
Subgenre: Action Adventure
Source:Based on Comic/Graphic Novel
Genre:Action
Production Method:Animation/Live Action
Creative Type:Historical Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Cruel and Unusual Films, Mark Canton, Gianni Nunnari
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

This new chapter of the epic saga takes the action to a fresh battlefield—on the sea—as Greek general Themistokles attempts to unite all of Greece by leading the charge that will change the course of the war. This pits Themistokles against the massive invading Persian forces led by mortal-turned-god Xerxes, and Artemisia, vengeful commander of the Persian navy.
